topic: New flagship topic: additive parallel-trends DiD versus odds-ratio equi-confounding DiD for binary outcomes. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchor is Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson 2024 Universal Difference-in-Differences, which replaces additive parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding for noncontinuous outcomes; adjacent anchors are Sofer et al. negative-control DiD and standard additive DiD decompositions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite observed 2x2x2 table frontier characterizing exactly when the additive DiD ATT, the odds-ratio/equi-confounding ATT, and a corrected scale-transport ATT coincide or diverge.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the finite observed binary DiD table frontier where additive parallel trends, odds-ratio equi-confounding, and corrected scale-transport identify the same ATT or provably diverge.

source_refs.claim: For binary, count, or polytomous outcomes, additive parallel trends may be implausible even with time-stable confounding; OREC is proposed as an alternative.
source_refs.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson

source_refs.claim: Universal DiD replaces parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding and identifies causal effects on target scales through a nonadditive restriction.
source_refs.author: Park and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: ordinary DiD and proximal DiD are two distinct exact-identification functionals
UniversalDID replaces additive parallel trends on outcome scale

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published UDiD line motivates OREC as a replacement for additive parallel trends on noncontinuous outcomes, while the prior CausalSmith DiD-bridge artifact flags UniversalDID as a distinct exact-identification functional; neither source gives a finite 2x2x2 observed-table iff condition describing the equality, strict-separation, and degeneracy regions among additive DiD, OREC DiD, and a corrected scale-transport ATT.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to convert additive mean-trend equality into an odds-ratio equality fails because UDiD makes the identifying restriction an odds-ratio confounding condition while the prior DiD artifact treats ordinary DiD and UniversalDID as distinct exact-identification functionals.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-support necessary-and-sufficient theorem giving observable algebraic conditions for equality, strict separation, and degeneracy of additive-PT, OREC, and corrected scale-transport ATT functionals.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct hand-checkable open-cell binary DiD witness families with identical observed pre/post margins where additive DiD is zero but OREC DiD is nonzero, and conversely.

source_refs.claim: UDiD argues that additive parallel trends can fail for bounded noncontinuous outcomes even when an odds-ratio equi-confounding restriction is more plausible.
source_refs.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson

source_refs.claim: In nonlinear DiD models, the interaction coefficient and the treatment effect are not the same observed cross-difference object.
source_refs.author: Puhani

source_refs.excerpt: moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law
Turn the displayed numerical witness into a symbolic open-cell certificate

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature supplies the motivation for scale-specific disagreement, and the prior reviewer record says the earlier non-nesting DiD attempt only gave moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law; the remaining gap is an explicit positive-cell family, not a single numerical logit example, certifying both directions of non-nesting under the same observed-margin constraints.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to exhibit one numerical 2x2 table fails because the reviewer record requires a full finite observed/latent law and an open-cell certificate rather than moment rows or an isolated table.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A parameterized binary 2x2x2 witness construction with positive cell probabilities and explicit polynomial inequalities certifying additive-only, OREC-only, both, and neither regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive a closed-form contamination term Delta_OR-add(P) that separates odds-ratio noncollapsibility, confounding drift, and true additive ATT drift in binary DiD.

source_refs.claim: Log-odds ratios are generally not comparable across groups, samples, time points, or model specifications.
source_refs.author: Mood

source_refs.claim: Differences between crude and conditional odds-ratio estimates can be decomposed into noncollapsibility and confounding components.
source_refs.author: Pang, Kaufman, and Platt

source_refs.claim: UDiD uses odds-ratio equi-confounding while still targeting interpretable causal effects such as additive ATT.
source_refs.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson

source_refs.excerpt: their difference has a first-order representation as a signed sum
State whether the flagship equality is full IF equality

open_problems.why_unsolved: Noncollapsibility papers identify why odds-ratio contrasts can move without causal confounding changes, and UDiD intentionally uses an odds-ratio confounding restriction to recover target-scale effects; prior CausalSmith nonlinear-DiD artifacts ask for signed contrast and full-equality objects, but none isolates a binary DiD contamination formula Delta_OR-add(P) mapping observed odds-ratio transport to additive ATT error.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to read a stable log-odds contrast as a stable additive bias fails because noncollapsibility sources show odds ratios change under conditioning and marginalization even without confounding, while the DiD frontier reviewers ask for explicit equality of the target functional rather than coefficient equality.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form Delta_OR-add(P) decomposition for finite binary DiD tables, with zero-set conditions under which odds-ratio transport and additive ATT transport coincide.

open_problems.open_problem: Give sharp finite-table partial-identification bounds for the binary ATT when bounded additive trend violations and bounded odds-ratio equi-confounding violations are imposed jointly.

source_refs.excerpt: the family identified set is \\(\\hat\\theta_\\Lambda-\\mathcal B_{\\Lambda,\\mathrm{TV}}
finite supports, each quantile statement becomes a pair of strict/weak linear inequalities
simplex constraints, and all adjacent odds-ratio divergence inequalities

open_problems.why_unsolved: Prior partial-ID DiD work developed support-function geometry for additive trend relaxations, while separate finite-support sensitivity artifacts developed simplex and adjacent odds-ratio divergence constraints; the joint binary DiD feasible body that combines additive and OREC slack has not been banked.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to intersect an additive-trend interval with an odds-ratio sensitivity interval fails because the prior finite-support artifacts formulate the OR side as simplex and likelihood-ratio constraints whose active faces can couple periods and arms rather than forming a product rectangle.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp LP or semi-algebraic support-function formula for the ATT identified set, reducing to exact additive DiD at zero additive slack and exact OREC DiD at zero odds-ratio slack.

open_problems.open_problem: Calibrate negative-control DiD for binary primary and control outcomes by characterizing when additive bias equivalence, odds-ratio bias equivalence, or a scale-spanning identified set is justified.

source_refs.claim: Control outcome calibration uses negative control outcomes to correct for unobserved confounding without assuming a simple treated-control comparison is unbiased.
source_refs.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Standard DiD can be read as a negative-outcome-control design relying on equal additive unmeasured-confounding bias.
source_refs.author: Sofer, Richardson, Colicino, Schwartz, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Double-negative-control methods identify causal effects through proxy or bridge conditions for unmeasured confounding.
source_refs.author: Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B

source_refs.claim: Bias equivalence is especially suspect when primary and negative-control outcomes differ in scale or bounds.
source_refs.author: Penning de Vries and Groenwold

open_problems.why_unsolved: Negative-control DiD papers explain the additive-bias reading of standard DiD, and proximal/bridge papers offer alternative confounding-control structure, but the binary-outcome case still lacks a simple finite-table criterion deciding whether the negative control should calibrate additive risk bias, odds-ratio bias, or a partially identified set spanning both scales.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to reuse additive negative-control bias equivalence fails because the negative-control literature flags scale and bound mismatches, and binary ceiling or floor effects can make additive bias small while odds-ratio confounding remains large.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite binary negative-control DiD theorem returning either a point-identified additive ATT under scale-calibrated equivalence or sharp sensitivity curves over additive and odds-ratio equi-confounding violations.

literature_map:
Athey and Imbens (2006, Econometrica) is the nonlinear DiD ancestor: changes-in-changes identifies counterfactual distributions and highlights scale dependence beyond additive mean trends.
Sofer, Richardson, Colicino, Schwartz, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2016, Statistical Science) recast standard DiD as a negative-outcome-control design based on equal additive confounding bias.
Tchetgen Tchetgen (2014, AJE) and Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2020, JRSSB) loosen simple negative-control bias equivalence through calibration and bridge/proxy structure.
Park and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2022, arXiv) and Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson (2024, Epidemiology) introduce Universal DiD / OREC for noncontinuous outcomes.
The key published tension is that UDiD uses an odds-ratio confounding restriction while many applied DiD targets are additive risk-difference ATTs.
Binary outcomes sharpen the issue because additive risk differences are bounded by baseline probabilities, while odds ratios are variation-dependent and noncollapsible.
Puhani (2012, Economics Letters) and Karaca-Mandic, Norton, and Dowd (2012, Health Services Research) clarify nonlinear DiD interaction mechanics but do not settle which identifying scale is credible.
Mood (2010), Norton (2012), Pang, Kaufman, and Platt (2016), and Westreich-Greenland-style discussions identify odds-ratio noncollapsibility as a separate obstacle from confounding.
Goodman-Bacon (2021), de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020), Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021), and Sun and Abraham (2021) give additive staggered-DiD decomposition and contamination anchors.
An OREC analogue of staggered decomposition and event-study diagnostics is not yet standard, especially after nonlinear aggregation back to risk differences.
Wooldridge (2023, Econometrics Journal) gives pragmatic nonlinear DiD estimators under familiar assumptions, but it is not a finite observed-table frontier for additive versus OREC restrictions.
The live gap for this topic is therefore the exact finite binary table frontier: iff equivalence, non-nesting witnesses, and a contamination map between additive and odds-ratio transport.
prior_proposal_map:
eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 is the closest prior: it juxtaposes ordinary additive DiD, proximal/negative-control DiD, UniversalDID, and non-nesting regimes.
Its reviewer JSON flags that earlier evidence used moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law, directly motivating open-cell witness families.
q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 supplies the estimator/frontier template but reviewers require non-tautological full IF or target equality.
eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1 warns that contrast matrices must be derived from estimator equations rather than assumed.
pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 and panel_honest_did_phase_v1 provide support-function geometry for relaxed additive parallel-trends DiD.
pid_collider_bias_sharp_v1 and pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 supply fixed-margin 2x2 odds-ratio algebra, bounded likelihood-ratio language, and open-cell witness cautions.
pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 contributes simplex plus adjacent odds-ratio divergence constraints useful for joint partial-ID bounds.
eid_cic_cl_quantile_nonequiv_v1 is a nonlinear-scale DiD analogue: useful for signed contrast structure, not itself a binary DiD result.
